obligations.reason: The debug interface (SimDTM/dmi_jtag/SimJTAG) through dm_slave_* signals can generate memory requests (dm_master_*) that may bypass normal access control. Need to verify whether debug requests are gated by authentication (jtag_unlock) and privilege checks, especially when debug_mode_i is active.
obligations.subject: debug interface (dm_slave → dm_master path)
obligations.operation: read/write to system bus via dm_master_*
obligations.object: arbitrary memory-mapped resources, including protected peripherals (fuses, bootrom, crypto) visible in the address map
obligations.expected_guard: jtag_unlock deasserted when not authenticated; debug access restricted by privilege state or locked by a sticky debug-disable bit
obligations.candidate_path: dm_slave_req → debug module → dm_master_req → noc2/noc3 interfaces toward peripherals (RSA, RNG, PKT, fuses, etc.)
required_evidence_slots: operation path dm_master_* to peripheral noc2/noc3
any explicit privilege comparators using priv_lvl_i
obligations.uncertainty: Whether debug master transactions are limited to certain address ranges and whether jtag_unlock gates all debug access is not yet known from static facts.

obligations.reason: The reglk_ctrl signal is a lock control vector for peripherals. Need to verify that each peripheral uses its lock bit to prevent writes to its security-critical registers, and that the lock cannot be cleared inadvertently or bypassed by debug_mode_i or testmode_i.
obligations.subject: CPU bus master (or debug master) writing to peripheral registers
obligations.operation: write to protected configuration/control registers of peripherals (RSA, RNG, PKT, etc.)
obligations.object: any register that holds keys, mode settings, or access-control policy
obligations.expected_guard: reglk_ctrl[peripheral_index] == 1 blocks writes; set-once or sticky, not clearable except by a secure reset
obligations.candidate_path: AXI write request → wrapper (rsa_wrapper, rng_wrapper, pkt_wrapper) → register write logic that uses reglk_ctrl_i input
state_conditions_to_check: reglk_ctrl bus value for each peripheral
whether reglk_ctrl can be cleared by debug, test, or CSR write
required_evidence_slots: write gating logic inside rsa_wrapper.sv, rng_wrapper.sv, pkt_wrapper.sv
obligations.uncertainty: The exact mapping of lock bits to peripherals and lock behavior (read-only after set, clear conditions) requires detailed RTL inspection.

obligations.reason: The fuse memory (fuse_mem) outputs jtag_hash and is accessed via pkt_wrapper using fuse_req_o/fuse_addr_o/fuse_rdata_i. Need to verify that read access to fuse contents is protected by appropriate guards such as reglk_ctrl or other authentication state, and that no debug or test path can bypass this protection.
obligations.subject: pkt_wrapper (as initiator for fuse reads) or any requester on the bus that generates transactions to pkt_wrapper
obligations.object: fuse memory internal storage (jtag_hash[255:0] and other fuse values)
obligations.expected_guard: access to fuses must require a specific lock state, authentication, or privilege level; debug/test should not be able to read fuses without the guard
obligations.candidate_path: bus request → pkt_wrapper → pkt module → fuse_mem (req_i, addr_i, rdata_o) → jtag_hash output; also debug path could attempt to read via dm_master to pkt_wrapper
state_conditions_to_check: whether pkt_wrapper fuse access is gated by lock or auth
required_evidence_slots: any lock or auth check before fuse_read
obligations.uncertainty: The role of pkey_loc_o in pkt.sv and its relation to fuse access protection is unclear; requires further investigation.

obligations.reason: The RNG wrapper (rng_wrapper) has a debug_mode_i input. Need to verify that when debug_mode is asserted, the internal random number state (entropy outputs, seed values) is not accessible or modifiable through the bus interface or any debug path, bypassing normal read restrictions.
obligations.subject: debug software executing in debug mode, or debug hardware access
obligations.operation: read or modify internal RNG state (entropy, seeds, control registers) through bus interface
obligations.object: RNG internal registers and output (rand_num_o, seed128_o, etc.)
obligations.expected_guard: debug_mode_i must disable read access to live random numbers or seeds, or a lock bit must prevent any access when debug is active; alternatively, RNG should be inaccessible in debug mode
obligations.candidate_path: debug_mode_i → rng_wrapper.sv → rng_top → rng_cs, rng_128, rng_64, rng_32, rng_16; bus access path via ariane_axi input
state_conditions_to_check: whether ariane_axi bus transactions are gated by debug_mode in rng_wrapper
obligations.uncertainty: The exact effect of debug_mode_i on RNG wrapper's bus interface is not specified in the facts. It may be benign or may expose internal state.

obligations.reason: The RSA wrapper (rsa_wrapper) also has a debug_mode_i input. Need to ensure that debug mode does not allow exposure of RSA keys (p_i, msg_in) or intermediate state, and that lock bits prevent unauthorized key writes.
obligations.subject: debug mode bus access through rsa_wrapper
obligations.operation: read key material or intermediate values from RSA registers
obligations.object: RSA internal key registers and computation state
obligations.expected_guard: debug_mode_i must block reads of key registers; or reglk_ctrl locks the key registers from any write; read-back of key registers should be disabled regardless of debug mode
obligations.candidate_path: debug_mode_i → rsa_wrapper.sv → rsa_top.v (encrypt_decrypt_i, p_i, msg_in, msg_out)
state_conditions_to_check: any write protection of key registers
direct access to p_i via bus reads
obligations.uncertainty: RSA wrapper may contain dedicated key registers that are read-only or not implemented; requires code review.

obligations.reason: The ariane_boot_sel_i selects between two bootrom instances. If the selection is not protected, an attacker may switch between bootroms at runtime to execute alternative code or read secure bootrom contents. Need to verify that boot_sel is locked after boot or that access to bootrom is disabled after boot.
obligations.subject: any bus master (CPU, debug) accessing bootrom
obligations.operation: read bootrom contents or execute code from bootrom
obligations.expected_guard: bootrom should be read-only and inaccessible after initial boot, or boot_sel must be locked/sticky after boot; debug must not be able to change boot_sel or read bootrom
obligations.candidate_path: ariane_boot_sel_i → mux for rom_req_acct → bootrom instance → rom_rdata
state_conditions_to_check: whether bootrom access is disabled via lock or address decode after boot
required_evidence_slots: boot_sel mux logic in riscv_peripherals.sv (lines 519-526, etc.)
any lock bit that blocks bootrom access after boot
debug's ability to reach bootrom address space
obligations.uncertainty: The bootrom is likely used only during reset; but static facts show no explicit lock. Requires checking lifecycle state machine.

obligations.reason: The priv_lvl_i input indicates CPU privilege level. Need to verify that MMIO access to security-critical peripherals (RSA, RNG, PKT, fuses, debug configuration) is restricted to appropriate privilege level (e.g., machine mode only), and that this check is enforced in the address decoder or wrapper.
obligations.subject: CPU bus master with priv_lvl_i
obligations.operation: read or write to peripheral registers
obligations.object: any peripheral mapped in the address space
obligations.expected_guard: access to certain peripherals must be limited to machine mode (priv_lvl_i == 2'b11) or require a specific permission; otherwise error/block
obligations.candidate_path: AXI request → address decoder in riscv_peripherals.sv → priv_lvl_i comparison before granting access to peripheral's noc2/noc3 interface
state_conditions_to_check: any privilege check logic in the address decode or peripheral wrapper
required_evidence_slots: address map for each noc2/noc3 interface
obligations.uncertainty: The static facts show priv_lvl_i is an input, but not how it is used. It may be unused, leading to missing privilege enforcement.

obligations.reason: The we_flag_[0-4] inputs suggest per-hart write-enable flags used for CSR access. Need to verify that these flags are used correctly to gate writes to sensitive CSRs, and that they cannot be manipulated by software in a way that bypasses protection.
obligations.subject: hart-specific write flags (we_flag_0..4)
obligations.operation: write to protected CSRs (e.g., mstatus, mcontrol) that should be locked
obligations.object: CSRs related to security, debug, or privilege
obligations.expected_guard: we_flag must be set appropriately (e.g., only by debug or machine mode) and must not be writable by untrusted code; after lock, we_flag should be sticky
obligations.candidate_path: we_flag inputs into riscv_peripherals.sv → debug or CSR logic → csr write enable
state_conditions_to_check: which CSRs use we_flag as write enable
whether we_flag can be cleared after set
required_evidence_slots: CSR write logic that uses these flags
any lock register that sets/clears we_flag
obligations.uncertainty: The purpose of we_flag signals is unclear without RTL code analysis; they might be part of a debug system's CSR write control.

obligations.reason: The presence of testmode_i input and multiple rst_* signals (rst_1..rst_13) suggests test and reset control. Need to verify that testmode does not reset or disable lock bits, and that partial resets do not leave security state uncleared (e.g., keys, lock bits, debug configuration).
obligations.subject: test mode or selective resets
obligations.operation: reset or clear of security state registers (lock bits, key registers) through test/reset signals
obligations.object: reglk_ctrl bits, acc_ctrl bits, key registers, RNG state, RSA keys
obligations.expected_guard: testmode_i must not affect security locks; resets should clear sensitive state; lock bits must not be cleared by any reset except power-on reset
obligations.candidate_path: testmode_i → combinational logic affecting lock bits or resets; rst_* signals → various modules (RSA, RNG, PKT, fuses)
state_conditions_to_check: whether RNG seeds are reset to known values
whether RSA key registers are cleared on reset
required_evidence_slots: each wrapper's reset sensitivity (rsa_wrapper, rng_wrapper, pkt_wrapper)
obligations.uncertainty: The purpose of multiple resets is unclear; they might be for different power domains. Need to verify no security degradation.

obligations.reason: The axi_lite_interface module is used for CLINT access. Need to verify that CLINT registers (timers, IPIs) are not writable by debug or lower privilege levels, and that no address aliasing allows unauthorized access.
obligations.subject: CLINT bus interface
obligations.operation: write to CLINT registers (mtimer, mtimecmp, msip)
obligations.expected_guard: access should be limited to machine mode (or as specified) and should not be accessible via debug unless allowed; address decoding must prevent aliasing
obligations.candidate_path: AXI → axi_lite_interface.sv → address decode → en_o, we_o; privilege check via priv_lvl_i or other
state_conditions_to_check: whether debug can access CLINT address range
address decode in axi_lite_interface.sv for overlapping regions
required_evidence_slots: debug's ability to reach CLINT address range
obligations.uncertainty: CLINT is usually not security-critical, but privilege bypass could allow unauthorized interrupts or timer manipulations.
